svg.title: A Unified View of LLM Distillation and Distillation Auditing
svg.desc: A detailed overview connecting teacher models, distillation signals, student models, audit access, evidence, conclusion levels, four supervision mechanisms, and representative 2025–2026 research directions.
svg.text: Figure 1. A Unified View of LLM Distillation and Distillation Auditing
Knowledge signals, teacher roles, student inheritance, audit access, and the ladder of claims that evidence can support.

text.tspan: Evidence may come from behavioral similarity, internal signals,
training artifacts, public disclosures, or provider telemetry.

g.text: ○ Role attribution: generator, judge, critic, filter
○ Provenance reconstruction of the full pipeline

svg.text: Illustration: How Different Distillation Signals Are Generated
A > B > N · scores · pairwise preferences · critiques
Student learns the teacher's preference boundary.
Conclusion Ladder: What We Can Claim

g.text: Recover the pipeline and data flow.

svg.text: Higher levels require stronger assumptions and more evidence.

text.tspan: Return a controlled set of plausible
sources instead of a forced winner.

svg.text: Source: created for the LLM Distillation & Lineage Observatory (distill.lightrain.asia). Original conceptual illustration.
References [1], [2], [3], [4], and [7] correspond to the site's master bibliography.
